items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Hmph! Don't look down on me.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: You'll regret it when you learn what I'm capable of!

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: That poker-faced researcher was absolutely shocked when she examined me! Said I'm not just any Echo, I'm a super duper cool one. Rarest of them all! Heh heh!

options.text: You sure you didn't add anything extra?
That doesn't sound like the Baizhi I know...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Yeah, no way Baizhi talks like that. She's more of a data dump kind of person. "Super duper cool"? Nah, too extra for her.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Seems like you're trying to boost your ego a bit!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Oh, actually... While those aren't Baizhi's exact words, that is what she meant.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: She mentioned highly intelligent Echoes that can act on their own in other countries. Said they are involved in every aspect of human life, with unique abilities beyond our imagination.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Those Echoes are rare though. Neither I nor Baizhi, an Ecoacoustics expert, have ever seen one in person. But this little thing here… It could be one of those foreign Echoes.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Yeah, makes sense! Now we just gotta figure out where it came from. Who knows, we may find other cool Echoes in that place!

options.text: Are you seriously buying all of this already?
Sure, sounds doable.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Well, both General Jiyan and Baizhi have confirmed it. No way they're lying.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: I'll admit, the thought of someone else having an Echo inside them sounds pretty crazy to me. But with you, anything's possible.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: You can absorb Reverberations with just your hands, like the legend says. So maybe one day you just stumbled upon this little thing and soaked it up like a sponge. Sounds feasible, I guess?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Sooo...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Do you know where exactly {Male=he;Female=she} absorbed you?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Huh? How am I supposed to know... Why don't you just ask {Male=him;Female=her}?

options.text: Because they know I don't remember.
Sure, she can ask. It's not like I have the answer.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Yeah. It happened before {Male=he;Female=she} lost all memories, so we have to ask you.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Tsk tsk... Don't take that tone with me.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: But... even if Echoes can have memories, they probably wouldn’t remember things earlier than their first manifestation. I wonder if that's the case with this little one, too?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Aha, finally someone with common sense.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: That green-haired serious guy asked me a similar question. Sorry to disappoint, but I really don't remember anything before I showed up.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Hmm. Maybe... maybe I was just sleeping inside {Male=him;Female=her} this whole time, so of course I don't remember!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Sleeping? Seriously? It's been so long! All those happenings, all that fighting, and you didn't hear anything? Well that's... Your sleeping quality is really something.

options.text: I'd prefer it to NOT stay awake all the time.
Uh, please no. There are things I don't want it to hear...

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Oh, so you've got privacy to be respected.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Don't worry! Your body's sound insulation is amazing. Dive into it and everything goes quiet.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: The only problem is, I never know when I'll wake up again. And when I do wake up, I get tired, hungry fast, so I have to crawl back in for more rest.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Hmm... I know! It's all because I'm not eating enough!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: That's why you kept disappearing. You went back to sleep from lack of energy. Makes sense. Regular Echoes need to be powered by the Terminal too.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hmm, I thought you'd be really different from the usual ones we see. Turns out you share a lot in common.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: So you probably don't know your denomination, or a nick name? No wonder everyone's been calling you the "little one"...

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Denomination? What's that?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: The universally agreed terms for special Echoes, like names for humans. They are named based on their characteristics, abilities, and places of origin.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: My denomination... It's... It's... I don't know. Do I not have a name at all?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: No way! Nooo way! That's not fair! If all the special Echoes have names, how can I not have one?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: I don't wanna be called the "little one" all the time. It doesn't sound cool at all!

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: How about this: you help me come up with a name, and I will let you have some of the food...

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: There's nothing left...

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Erm... Next time! Next time I'll definitely save some for you! Just... give me a name! Please!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: A name? Now?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Yes, I want it now! Look, your name's Chixia, your name's Yangyang, and you... Uhhh, your name is...

options.text: {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: {PlayerName}? ...That sounds interesting. Wait, didn't you forget everything? How do you still remember...

options.text: I just came up with it. It's my new name now.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: With your old name and memories all gone, it's a good idea to go with a new one. It makes everything more convenient and represents a fresh start.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Yeah, exactly like she said! Every one of you has a name, and I want one for myself too! Really not asking much.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: I just want a name that sounds a little cool, a little special and epic and super smokin'!

options.text: That's a long list of descriptors...
Yeah, you having a name should make things easier.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Names are a big deal, you know? Like, once you have one, it's stuck with you for life. Gotta make sure it's a good one!

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Can't have people not scared of me when they hear it. No time for regrets here!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: That's true.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Let's see... You want a cool one... What about "Echo the Invincible"? What do you say?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Nah, nuh-uh, absolutely no! That's too straightforward. It's-It's no better than calling me the "little one"!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hey, it makes every difference in the world! "I am Echo the Invincible!" That's what a Hero Play character would say as their transformation call!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Or maybe... Since you can fly and you've got those long ears... why don’t you call yourself... "Righteous Raptor"? Or "Valor Hawk"? "Flying Fury"?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: No, absolutely no. Why do they all sound so... cringy...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Why? I love it when people call me the Jinzhou Speedster. Doesn't that sound awesome?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Uh... Haha, sure, if you say so... Anyway, they all sound like anything but my name. ABSOLUTELY NO!

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Aha! I knew {PlayerName} would come up with a good... Wait. Why does it sound so random?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: You didn't just pick two random syllables, did you?

options.text: Why not? Simplicity works wonders.
Abby means joy. It's what you bring to us.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: I mean... Yeah, it's simple, easy to remember. l can imagine myself yelling it out loud, and at least it sounds better than Chixia’s cringy titles... But you know, it doesn’t feel quite majestic enough...

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: So... Is it because I've been saying "Absolutely no" a lot...?

options.text: (Why, isn't that a nice name..)
(Maybe I should come up with a better one...)

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: (Hmm... Why is it suddenly so quiet? Is it not happy with the name? Why though... Can't think of anything better that's catchy, impactful, and clever all at once.)

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: (Uh, I meant to tease it as a joke, but I can tell it's upset now. On second thought, a name is indeed very important. Maybe I'll have to come up with a different one...)

options.text: Let’s just think of another...

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Hmm... Abby... Abra... Abraxas?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: What's wrong? What are you muttering about? Abra... what? Sounds like you're reading a spell.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: ...Erm, I don't know, but... I just have this feeling that... this is what my name should be. Okay, ready?

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Abby! I like the sound of that. That's my name!

options.text: I thought you didn't like it.
Good. Saves us time thinking of another one.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: Of course I like it! You came up with it for me! I was just... trying to get used to it, that's all! Besides, I feel attached to this name now.

items.speaker: "Little One"
items.text: My name is Abby. You will NOT call me the "little one" again!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Sure, we won't. Got it.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Well, that didn't work out. I was hoping we could get some answers from the little... I mean, from Abby, but now we're back at square one.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: I really thought we could figure out where Abby came from. It might not lead us to other special Echoes, but it's at least a starting point to uncover {PlayerName}'s past. Then we'll have something to do before asking Madam Magistrate and our Sentinel about it again.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Maybe we can start with Abby's special abilities instead?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Each special Echo has a unique ability. We can compare what Abby does with our records of other Echoes to see where they came from. Besides… It was Abby’s power that helped {PlayerName} defeat the Threnodian, I suppose?

options.text: Likely so.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Why do you sound so unsure? Didn't you see it all with your own eyes?

options.text: It happened too fast. I didn't get a clear look.
I'd be more sure if I can see it again.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hmm… Abby, can you show us again? I'm super curious how you did that. Who knows, we might learn something.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Well, since you asked, I'll show you how it's done! But... this place is too crowded. Let's move to that open spot over there.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Alright. Ahem! All eyes on me!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: W-what's going on? I... I'll try again! Just you see!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Uh... What? Did something happen?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: J-Just a little slip, that's all! I can do it! You gotta trust me!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Back then, I just stood in front of {Male=him;Female=her}, and that big bad Threnodian monster thing just... just... left.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: So... you didn't really do anything?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Huh? No, I... I definitely did something!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Like I said, I was asleep, and then all of a sudden, I smelled something really yummy coming closer and closer… It was like nothing I ever smelled before!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I didn't have time to think. I just had to show up and reach out for it.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: So... maybe Abby's power activates automatically under certain circumstances? Perhaps Abby can’t control it yet.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Yeah, it looks that way. You can't even hold your shape for very long yet...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hey, hey! Stop looking down on me! Like I said, it was just a little slip, really!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: But think about it! I just stood there and did nothing and ended up beating a THRENODIAN! Imagine what I could do if I actually tried!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hey, {PlayerName}! Get behind me next time we run into anything, 'kay? I'll keep you safe, promise!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: You're so real for that, Abby.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: You bet! I said I'm super strong. I'll protect {Male=him;Female=her}!

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: That's very reassuring to hear. But... it seems we're stuck again. I can't think of anything else to check out.

options.text: Let’s take our time. There's no hurry.
We'll still need to figure out my past first.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Knew it. Nothing about {PlayerName}'s ever gonna be easy to figure out... Hmm. Maybe we should bring Baizhi, Mr. Xiangli Yao, and all the researchers in Jinzhou... No, in the entire Huanglong together. They'll do a nice and thorough examination on Abby, and then...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: ABSOLUTELY NO!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I said NO! Told that poker-face researcher already!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I'll make it clear. I am to stay with {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: I agree. Abby cannot leave {PlayerName}'s side, but we can't just trap {Male=him;Female=her} here for research.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Where did you absorb Abby, what are Abby's powers, and what exactly happened between you two... There are so many questions we can't figure out yet.

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Our Sentinel Jué can look into the future. Nothing ever deviates from its predictions. It has already sort of guided you to the Norfall Barrens through Madam Magistrate's messages, right?

items.speaker: Yangyang
items.text: Now that the Threnodian crisis is over, perhaps you can consult our Magistrate and our Sentinel again. I’m sure they can offer you some more useful guidance.

options.text: I'll pay Jinhsi another visit once she returns.
We'll have to wait for now.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Relax, relax! You have me now, remember? Meeting up with that Jinhsi person? Getting your memories back? I've got you!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Speaking of that, so this Sentinel can predict the future? That sounds cool. The name "Jué" sounds pretty cool too. It's almost as cool as mine!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: What does it look like? Where is it? Since we're paying it a visit, this Sentinel should treat us with food, right? I wonder how the food's gonna taste...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Our Sentinel protects every one of us. Of course it's cool!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: But... why are you talking about food again. Didn't you just stuff your face? What? Is your stomach a black hole?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I can't help it. I'm always starving…
After parting ways with Yangyang and Chixia, you travel around Jinzhou with Abby while waiting for replies from Jinhsi, trying to look for more clues about your past.
Several days later...
